tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Define the Stage 1 accessibility settings profile
tasks.summary: Define a device-independent, versioned profile for required Stage 1 comfort and readability settings.
tasks.objective: Create one validated settings model consumed by adapters and evidence without changing player-intent or diagnostics contracts.
inputs: Consumed contracts: ContentVersion/BuildIdentity v1, Player Intents v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; accepted evidence configuration and input lifecycle conventions.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: shared contracts, input action assets, gameplay tuning, scenes, prefabs, generated registries, persistence, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none; AR-005 owns the separate settings schema. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove four owned files before consumers land. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: The profile represents reduced effects, flash/shake limits, warning redundancy, audio levels and input comfort without device-specific gameplay intent.
Unknown versions, invalid ranges and missing required settings fail deterministically.
Defaults are practical, offline-capable and preserve color-independent warnings.
verification: Automated: defaults, range, unknown-version, round-trip value and required-setting tests.
Manual: accessibility contract review confirms every accepted Stage 1 control has one owner.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-002 and AR-005 consume this model; do not duplicate settings authority.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.25 focused lead day allocated to the remaining S1.3 reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Apply reduced-effects and comfort settings
tasks.summary: Apply the validated profile to temporary Stage 1 presentation through isolated Unity adapters.
tasks.objective: Control flash, shake, particles and warning intensity without changing combat, weapon or enemy-owned assets.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; AR-001 profile, temporary weapon presentation and enemy/route evidence fixtures.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: weapon/enemy packages, scenes, effects assets, combat/movement foundations, shared settings model and persistence.
Serialized ownership: none beyond transient test objects. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three owned files. Prototype debt: adapter targets temporary Stage 1 presentation and must be reviewed when final effects replace it.
acceptance_criteria: Every supported setting applies at runtime and reset restores deterministic defaults.
Reduced effects lower visual intensity without removing timing, shape, textural or audio warning information.
Missing optional effect targets fail safely and are diagnosed without invalidating unrelated controls.
verification: Automated: profile application, minimized-effects, missing-target, reset and restart tests.
Manual/playable: compare default and reduced-effects scenarios without editing package assets.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-003 and AR-008 consume this adapter read-only.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.35 focused lead day allocated to the remaining S1.3 reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Validate color-independent warnings and readability
tasks.summary: Audit Stage 1 attacks, hazards and status warnings for redundant non-color cues.
tasks.objective: Provide an executable warning inventory and fail evidence when required cues depend on color alone.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; accepted weapon/enemy human reviews, package presentation and reduced-effects adapter.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: gameplay/presentation implementation, scenes, packages, shared adapters, tuning and registries.
Serialized ownership: warning inventory fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three owned files. Prototype debt: inventory tracks temporary-cue replacement obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Every representative attack, hazard, fallback and boss warning has at least one timing/shape/motion/audio cue independent of color.
Reduced-effects mode preserves critical warning onset and duration.
Missing, ambiguous or color-only cues deterministically fail the matrix.
verification: Automated: inventory completeness, cue redundancy, reduced-effects and missing-reference tests.
Manual/playable: review fixed scenarios with color desaturation and reduced effects.
tasks.handoff_notes: Blocking findings become bounded follow-up tasks; this audit does not edit cues.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.35 focused lead day allocated to the remaining S1.3 reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Verify device, focus-loss, and disconnect behavior
tasks.summary: Exercise movement and combat intent across keyboard/mouse, gamepad, focus loss and device disconnects.
tasks.objective: Prove lifecycle safety and device-independent intent without editing either input action asset.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Player Intents v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; movement/combat adapters, restart harness and enemy/route evidence.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: both inputactions assets, adapters, ProjectSettings, gameplay, scenes and packages.
Serialized ownership: lifecycle fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three owned files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Held movement/fire/thruster intents clear safely on focus loss, disconnect, disable and restart.
Keyboard/mouse and gamepad produce equivalent device-independent intent semantics.
Reconnect cannot replay stale presses or strand an active attack.
verification: Automated: focus, disconnect, reconnect, disable, held-action and rapid-restart matrix tests.
Manual/playable: complete representative arena cases with each supported device family.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-006 consumes this matrix for repeated-session reliability.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.30 focused lead day allocated to the remaining S1.3 reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Persist Stage 1 settings safely
tasks.summary: Persist accessibility and comfort settings atomically in a separate local settings document.
tasks.objective: Add versioned offline settings persistence without introducing MissionRunState, campaign-save or reward authority.
inputs: Consumed contracts: ContentVersion/BuildIdentity v1; bootstrap/restart lifecycle and AR-001 accessibility profile.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: MissionRunState, campaign saves, rewards, PlayerPrefs, scenes, bootstrap ownership, packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: no campaign-save impact; exclusively defines local settings-document v1. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove four files and return to defaults. Prototype debt: settings store must later integrate through the Stage 2 persistence composition root without changing document semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Writes use temp-file plus atomic replacement and preserve the last valid document on interruption.
Missing, corrupt or unsupported documents fall back safely with bounded local diagnostics.
Settings survive restart while gameplay, mission, reward and shop state are never serialized.
verification: Automated: round-trip, missing, corrupt, unsupported-version, interrupted-write and atomic-replacement tests.
Manual: clean local profile check confirms offline operation and documented recovery location.
tasks.handoff_notes: This is settings-only authority; Stage 2 save tasks must not silently absorb or rewrite it.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.40 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Run repeated-session and rapid-restart reliability tests
tasks.summary: Exercise complete arena and short-route sessions repeatedly under lifecycle stress.
tasks.objective: Detect stale actors, inputs, projectiles, diagnostics and settings across bounded repeat/restart runs.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; restart/smoke harness, weapon/enemy evidence, input lifecycle matrix and settings store.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: gameplay, scenes, evidence framework, settings/input implementation, tuning and registries.
Serialized ownership: repeated-session fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three files. Prototype debt: bounded Stage 1 soak duration must be replaced by the complete Stage 2 matrix.
acceptance_criteria: The frozen run count completes without stale state, duplicate completion or settings drift.
Rapid restart clears actors, contacts, projectiles, held intents and diagnostics boundaries deterministically.
Every technical failure marks the session invalid rather than counting as gameplay evidence.
verification: Automated: repeated arena/route, restart-during-attack, settings-reload and completion-once tests.
Manual: run the documented bounded soak and inspect resource/diagnostic trends.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-007 classifies failures; do not hide failures by lowering run count.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.45 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Validate diagnostics and invalid-session classification
tasks.summary: Verify bounded diagnostics and objective classification of technically invalid Stage 1 sessions.
tasks.objective: Ensure reliability failures remain visible and cannot be counted as fun or behavioral evidence.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; diagnostics recorder, evidence manifests/protocol and repeated-session results.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: diagnostics implementation/contracts, evidence generator, gameplay, scenes, settings and tuning.
Serialized ownership: validity fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Every declared technical failure maps deterministically to an invalid reason and bounded diagnostic evidence.
Valid sessions remain valid under benign warnings and diagnostics never grow without bounds.
Manifest identity links every classification to the exact build/content/tuning configuration.
verification: Automated: failure injection, benign warning, overflow, identity mismatch and classification-once tests.
Manual: reviewer follows the matrix on valid and intentionally invalid evidence packages.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-011 may include only sessions classified through this matrix.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.35 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Run representative combat and enemy stress tests
tasks.summary: Exercise bounded high-density Stage 1 movement, weapons, enemies and effects against recorded budgets.
tasks.objective: Produce comparable performance evidence without changing gameplay counts or hiding failures through reduced effects.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; performance hooks and representative movement/combat/weapon/enemy evidence.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: gameplay/effect implementation, scenes, performance probe, tuning, quality settings, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: stress fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three files. Prototype debt: Stage 1 density bounds must be superseded by Stage 2 full matrices.
acceptance_criteria: Frozen default and reduced-effects scenarios exercise representative projectile, enemy, hazard and warning density.
Frame-time, allocation and diagnostic budgets are captured with build/content/tuning identity.
A budget miss fails evidence and cannot be concealed by reducing authored encounter density.
verification: Automated: stress entrypoint, budget, allocation, identity and reduced-effects comparison tests.
Manual: inspect captures for spikes, warning loss and unbounded object growth.
tasks.handoff_notes: Budget failures become explicit blockers or bounded follow-up tasks.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.45 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Verify clean-machine Windows builds and smoke tests
tasks.summary: Verify a reproducible Windows development build from a clean checkout and local toolchain.
tasks.objective: Prove offline clean-machine build, settings recovery and representative smoke entrypoints with immutable identity.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; Windows build/smoke baseline, manifests, settings, validity and stress results.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: existing build scripts, Unity project/package settings, CI workflows, gameplay, scenes, generated registries and release signing.
Serialized ownership: clean-machine fixture only. Save-schema impact: exercises settings v1 only. Content-registry impact: verifies generated outputs without editing them.
Rollback: remove four files. Prototype debt: local clean-machine wrapper must later be covered by complete release matrices.
acceptance_criteria: A clean checkout with the pinned editor/packages builds and runs required smoke entrypoints without network services.
Artifacts include matching build/content/tuning identity, manifest checksum and bounded diagnostics.
Missing generated output, corrupt settings or smoke failure stops verification with actionable local output.
verification: Automated: run contract tests and clean-machine wrapper from a disposable checkout.
Manual: launch the resulting Windows build and complete the documented smoke path.
tasks.handoff_notes: The wrapper orchestrates accepted entrypoints; it must not fork their logic.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.45 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Execute the Stage 1 accessibility matrix
tasks.summary: Run the complete practical accessibility matrix over accepted weapon and enemy/route review cases.
tasks.objective: Record evidence for comfort, controls, warnings, audio and reduced-effects coverage without changing implementation.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; accepted weapon/enemy human gates, warning/input matrices and persistent settings.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all implementation, tuning, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, settings store and registries.
Serialized ownership: accessibility matrix fixture only. Save-schema impact: reads settings v1 only. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three files. Prototype debt: practical Stage 1 matrix is not a claim of exhaustive disability certification.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix covers keyboard/mouse, gamepad, reduced effects, audio reduction, color-independent warnings and settings restart.
Required gameplay information remains available through redundant cues in representative complete sessions.
Discomfort, ambiguity or technical invalidity is recorded as a blocker, not averaged away.
verification: Automated: coverage, required-setting, evidence-link and valid-session tests.
Manual/playable: execute the documented matrix and record comfort/readability outcomes.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-011 consumes only completed, valid matrix rows.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.45 focused lead day allocated to the remaining S1.3 reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Assemble the immutable Stage 1 readiness evidence
tasks.summary: Assemble accessibility, reliability, diagnostics, performance and clean-build results into one immutable package.
tasks.objective: Produce checksum-linked gate inputs while preserving valid/invalid session distinctions and source identities.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; accepted evidence protocol and every AR matrix/result.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: evidence sources, gameplay, scenes, settings, build implementation, generated registries and gate decisions.
Serialized ownership: readiness package fixture only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove four files; source evidence remains intact. Prototype debt: Stage 1-only package format expires after Stage 2 matrix replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Package deterministically links every required result, identity, checksum and invalid-session exclusion.
Missing, stale, mismatched or failed evidence prevents a ready package.
Assembler never modifies source evidence or turns a blocker into a pass.
verification: Automated: complete, missing, stale, checksum mismatch, invalid-session and deterministic rebuild tests.
Manual: independent reviewer traces sampled package entries back to source artifacts.
tasks.handoff_notes: AR-012 summarizes this package; GATE tasks remain the approval authority.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.50 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: can representative complete sessions remain accessible, diagnostically trustworthy, performant and reliable enough to enter the formal human gate?
tasks.title: Produce the Stage 1 gate-readiness report
tasks.summary: Summarize objective readiness and blockers for the separate human Stage 1 gate batch.
tasks.objective: Create a machine-checkable pass/block report without deciding the developer or external behavioral gate.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; accepted weapon/enemy human gates and immutable AR-011 readiness package.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all implementation, tuning, evidence sources, planning, task index, collaboration state and gate acceptance records.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove two report files and return Stage 1 gate to blocked. Prototype debt: none.
This task reports evidence only; GATE tasks own developer/external decisions and any iteration authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Every required accessibility, reliability, diagnostic, performance, clean-build and human-review input is pass or explicit blocker.
Report fingerprints match AR-011 and preserve all failed/invalid findings.
The report clearly states that it is not Stage 1 approval.
verification: Automated: validate report schema, required dimensions, fingerprints, blocker propagation and AR-011 links.
Manual: reviewer cross-checks every summary row against the immutable package.
edge_cases: all technical checks pass but feel gate fails
tasks.handoff_notes: The next stage1-gate batch must depend on this report and independently execute human decisions.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.30 focused lead day allocated to S1.4. Batch direct spend totals 4.60 focused lead days: 1.70 from remaining S1.3 reserve and 2.90 from S1.4, leaving 2.55 S1.3 days and 2.10 S1.4 days for review and the developer gate.
